settings.name: build_type%
settings.justification: Upstream release artifacts use the Release configuration.

settings.name: node_shared
settings.justification: Upstream release artifacts link the Node.js executable instead of a shared libnode library.

settings.name: node_use_node_snapshot
settings.justification: Upstream same-architecture release builds embed the Node.js startup snapshot; Bazel must preserve the snapshot for every target platform.

settings.name: node_use_node_code_cache
settings.justification: Upstream release builds embed the built-in JavaScript code cache.

settings.name: node_write_snapshot_as_array_literals
settings.justification: Upstream Linux and macOS builds use the non-array snapshot representation.

settings.name: node_use_amaro
settings.justification: Upstream release builds include Amaro TypeScript support.

settings.name: node_use_bundled_v8
settings.justification: Node.js 26.3.1 release artifacts use the V8 14.6.202.34 source bundled in the Node.js archive.

settings.name: node_use_openssl
settings.justification: Upstream release artifacts include the bundled OpenSSL implementation.

settings.name: node_use_sqlite
settings.justification: Upstream release artifacts include the bundled SQLite implementation.

settings.name: node_use_ffi
settings.justification: Node.js 26.3.1 release artifacts enable the bundled libffi implementation on Linux and macOS x86_64 and arm64 targets.

settings.name: node_use_lief
settings.justification: Node.js 26.3.1 release artifacts enable bundled LIEF for single-executable application support on Linux and macOS.

settings.name: node_use_v8_platform
settings.justification: Node.js uses the V8 platform implementation for foreground and worker tasks.

settings.name: v8_enable_i18n_support
settings.justification: The default --with-intl=full-icu release configuration enables V8 internationalization.

settings.name: v8_enable_temporal_support
settings.justification: The full-ICU release configuration enables Temporal by default.

settings.name: v8_enable_webassembly
settings.justification: The default release configuration does not use V8 lite mode and enables WebAssembly.

settings.name: v8_enable_maglev
settings.justification: Node.js enables Maglev by default on the supported x64 and arm64 release architectures.

settings.name: v8_enable_pointer_compression
settings.justification: Node.js disables V8 pointer compression by default; the Bazel V8 build must override V8's x64 and arm64 default.

settings.name: v8_enable_pointer_compression_shared_cage
settings.justification: Node.js disables the shared pointer-compression cage by default.

settings.name: v8_enable_sandbox
settings.justification: Node.js explicitly disables the V8 sandbox because Node.js ArrayBuffer backing stores are not sandbox-compatible.

settings.name: force_dynamic_crt
settings.justification: The non-shared release configuration does not force a dynamic C runtime.

settings.name: target_arch
settings.justification: Node.js and V8 use x64 for x86_64 targets and arm64 for AArch64 targets.
